scenes.name: Title
scenes.text: Human in the loop. [pause] The teacher decides. The AI assists.

scenes.name: Principle
scenes.text: The term comes from engineering and safety. [pause] A human must approve, verify, or intervene before the system proceeds. [pause] In aviation. In medicine. And now — in education.

scenes.name: Why
scenes.text: Three reasons education needs a human in the loop. [pause] AI makes errors. AI lacks context. [pause] And education is — at its core — a human relationship.

scenes.name: Levels
scenes.text: Oversight works at three levels. [pause] Before — review what AI produces. During — monitor student-AI interactions. After — evaluate outcomes. [pause] Most classrooms need before and after. Real-time when students are young.

scenes.name: Functions
scenes.text: Four functions only a teacher provides. [pause] Emotional attunement. Ethical judgement. Curriculum integration. Accountability. [pause] None of these can be delegated to a machine.

scenes.name: Workload
scenes.text: But oversight cannot become a burden. [pause] Build it into existing workflows. Sample student-AI transcripts. Add reflection prompts. [pause] Sustainable oversight beats heroic oversight.

scenes.name: Quote
scenes.text: Remember — clicking 'approve' without reading is not oversight. [pause] The principle requires genuine engagement with what AI produces. [pause] Presence is not enough.

scenes.name: Checklist
scenes.text: Before students see AI content — six quick questions. [pause] Is it accurate? Unbiased? Age-appropriate? Aligned with my objective? Culturally sensitive? Defensible to a parent?
